name: Yule Log
recipeInstructions:
1. Brush the bottom and sides of a 15x10 inch jellyroll pan with vegetable oil and line with wax paper.
2. Set aside.
3. Beat egg yolks at high speed with an electric mixer until thick and pale.
4. Gradually add 1/4 cup sugar, beating constantly.
5. Add 1 tablespoon oil and almond extract, stirring well.
6. Beat egg whites until foamy; gradually add remaining 1/2 cup sugar, beating until stiff but not dry.
7. Fold the egg whites into the yolk mixture.
8. Combine flour, baking powder, and salt; gradually fold into egg mixture.
9. Spread batter evenly into prepared pan.
10. Bake at 350°F for 8 minutes or until the center of the cake springs back when touched.
11. Sift powdered sugar over a 15x10 inch rectangle on a cloth towel. When done baking, loosen from sides of pan and turn out onto towel.
12. Carefully peel off wax paper. Starting at the narrow end, roll the cake and towel together in jelly roll fashion; cool completely on a wire rack, seam side down.
13. Unroll the cake and spread with 1 cup Mocha Buttercream Frosting. Carefully reroll without towel. Place seam side down on a serving plate.
14. Spread remaining frosting over the cake.
15. Diagonally cut a 1 inch slice from one end of the cake, and place against the top center to resemble the knot of a tree.
16. Score frosting with fork tines to resemble bark.
17. Chill at least 1 hour before serving.
Makes 8-10 servings.
